---
title: "SO_R020: Supplemental Description Notation Rule"
subtitle: "All supplemental descriptions in filenames MUST use the format `basename_description.extension`"
chapter: "CH01"
category: "rule"
number: "SO_R020"
date-created: "2025-08-18"
date-modified: "2025-08-18"
author: "Claude"
type: "rule"
law: "Structure & Organization Law"
article-number: 
  number: 20
  title: "Supplemental Description Notation Rule"
related_to:
  - "R001: File Naming Convention"
  - "R028: Archiving Standard"
  - "R069: Function File Naming"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Article 20: Supplemental Description Notation Rule {#overview}

**Structure & Organization Law Article 20 (Supplemental Description Notation Rule)**

This rule establishes a standard notation for supplemental descriptions in filenames.

:::{.callout-important}
## Core Rule
All supplemental descriptions in filenames MUST use the format `basename_description.extension`.
:::

# Section 1: Description {#description}

This rule establishes a standard notation for supplemental descriptions in filenames. When a file variant needs to be distinguished from its original or primary version, the supplemental description must be separated from the base filename using an underscore character (_).

# Section 2: Rationale {#rationale}

## Subsection 1: Consistency {#consistency}

Provides a uniform pattern for supplemental descriptions across the codebase.

## Subsection 2: Clarity {#clarity}

Makes it immediately clear which part of the filename is the base and which part is a supplemental description.

## Subsection 3: Searchability {#searchability}

Makes it easier to find all variants of a file by searching for the base name.

## Subsection 4: Sorting {#sorting}

Files with the same base name will be grouped together in directory listings.

## Subsection 5: Compatibility {#compatibility}

Uses underscores which are widely compatible across operating systems and file systems.

# Section 3: Implementation Guide {#implementation-guide}

## Subsection 1: Correct Format {#correct-format}

```
basename_description.extension
```

For example:
- `microCustomer_R0076.R` (indicating compliance with Rule 76)
- `config_development.yaml` (indicating a development configuration)
- `report_monthly.pdf` (indicating a monthly report)

## Subsection 2: Multi-part Descriptions {#multi-part-descriptions}

For multi-part descriptions, use additional underscores to separate the parts:

```
basename_part1_part2.extension
```

For example:
- `dataProcessor_v2_beta.R` (version 2, beta release)
- `order_2023_summary.csv` (2023 summary of orders)

## Subsection 3: Incorrect Formats {#incorrect-formats}

Do not use any of these formats:

```
basename.description.extension   # Wrong: uses dots instead of underscores
basename-description.extension   # Wrong: uses hyphens instead of underscores
basenameDescription.extension    # Wrong: uses camelCase instead of underscores
basename (description).extension # Wrong: uses parentheses
```

## Subsection 4: File Versioning {#file-versioning}

For version-based supplemental descriptions, use a consistent format:

```
basename_v1.extension
basename_v2.extension
```

## Subsection 5: Rules vs. Implementation {#rules-vs-implementation}

The supplemental description should indicate what is different about the file, not just label it with an arbitrary code:

```r
# CORRECT (descriptive)
dataProcessor_optimized.R
dataProcessor_parallel.R

# INCORRECT (non-descriptive)
dataProcessor_1.R
dataProcessor_new.R
```

## Subsection 6: Special Case - Temporary Files {#temporary-files}

For temporary files, use a consistent prefix:

```
basename_temp.extension
basename_backup.extension
```

# Section 4: Examples {#examples}

## Subsection 1: Correct Examples {#correct-examples}

- `microCustomer_R0076.R` - microCustomer implementation that follows Rule 76
- `app_production.R` - Production version of app.R
- `config_development.yaml` - Development configuration
- `report_2023_Q1.pdf` - Q1 2023 report
- `database_backup_2023_01_15.sql` - Database backup from January 15, 2023

## Subsection 2: Incorrect Examples {#incorrect-examples}

- `microCustomerR0076.R` - Missing underscore separator
- `app-production.R` - Uses hyphen instead of underscore
- `config.dev.yaml` - Uses dots instead of underscores
- `report(2023).pdf` - Uses parentheses instead of underscores

# Section 5: Benefits {#benefits}

Following this rule will:

1. **Improve File Organization**: Related files naturally group together
2. **Simplify File Management**: Easy to identify and manage file variants
3. **Enhance Readability**: File purpose is immediately clear
4. **Improve Automation**: Scripts can reliably parse filenames
5. **Reduce Confusion**: Avoids ambiguity in file naming

---

*Based on: Structure & Organization Law Article 20*  
*Related to: [R001 File Naming Convention](./SO_R002_file_naming_convention.qmd), [R069 Function File Naming](../../CH03_development_methodology/rules/DEV_R018_function_file_naming.qmd)*
